title: Databases - UGC NET CSE  June 2014  Part 2  Question - Theoretical/Not Answered

sections.name: UGC NET CSE  June 2014  Part 2  Question

questions.question:
Data security threats include



UGC NET CSE | December 2011 | Part 2 | Question: 23

Which one of the following pairs is correctly matched in the context of database design ?

\( \begin{array}{|l|l|l|l|}\hline &  \text{List-I(Database term)} && \text{List-II(Definition)}  \\\hline \text{I.} & \text{Specialization}  & \text{A.} & \text{Result of taking the union of two or more} \\ &&&\text{disjoint (lower-level) entity sets to produce} \\&&&\text{a higher-level entity set.} \\\hline \text{II.} & \text{Generalization}  & \text{B.} & \text{Express the number of entities to which} \\ &&& \text{another entity can be associated via a} \\ &&& \text{relationship set.} \\\hline \text{III.} & \text{Aggregation}  & \text{C.} & \text{Result of taking a subset of a higher-level} \\ &&& \text{entity set to form a lower-level entity set.} \\\hline \text{IV.} & \text{Mapping cardinality}  & \text{D.} & \text{An abstraction in which relationship sets} \\&&& \text{(along with their associated entity sets) are} \\ &&& \text{treated as higher-level entity sets, and can} \\ &&& \text{participate in relationships. } \\\hline \end{array} \)

Codes :



UGC NET CSE | August 2016 | Part 3 | Question: 12

(i) \( \text{DML} \) includes a query language based on both relation algebra and tuple calculus

(ii) \( \text{DML} \) includes a query language based on tuple calculus

(iii) \( \text{DML} \) includes a query language based on relational algebra

(iv) \( \text{DML} \) includes a query language based on none of the relational algebra and tuple calculus

Which one is correct ?



UGC NET CSE | June 2009 | Part 2 | Question: 18

Which construct in SQL is used to test whether a subquery has any tuples in its result ?



UGC NET CSE | June 2009 | Part 2 | Question: 21

ORACLE supports :



UGC NET CSE | June 2009 | Part 2 | Question: 22
options: inner join, outer join , semi join only
inner join, outer join, semi join and anti join

questions.question:
Database systems that store each relation in a separate operating system file may use the operating system's authorization scheme, instead of defining a special scheme themselves. In this case, which of the following is false?



UGC NET CSE | July 2018 | Part 2 | Question: 68
options: The administrator enjoys more control on the grant option
It is difficult among the update, delete and insert authorizations
cannot store more than one relation in a file
Operations on the database are speeded up as the authorization procedure is carried out at the operating system level

questions.question:
The entity type on which the __________ type depends is called the identifying owner.



UGC NET CSE | December 2004 | Part 2 | Question: 19

The completeness constraint has rules :



UGC NET CSE | December 2004 | Part 2 | Question: 18

Specialization is a __________ process.



UGC NET CSE | December 2004 | Part 2 | Question: 17

The \( \text{E-R} \) model is expressed in terms of :

  1. Entities

  2. The relationship among entities

  3. The attributes of the entities

Then



UGC NET CSE | December 2004 | Part 2 | Question: 16

Two phase protocol in a database management system is :



UGC NET CSE | December 2006 | Part 2 | Question: 20
options: a concurrency mechanism that is not deadlock free
a recovery protocol used for restoring a database after a crash
Any update to the system log done in 2-phases

questions.question:
In DBMS, deferred update means :



UGC NET CSE | December 2006 | Part 2 | Question: 17
options: All the updates are done first but the entries are made in the log file later
All the log files entries are made first but the actual updates are done later
Every update is done first followed by a writing on the log file
Changes in the views are deferred till a query asks for a view

questions.question:
Consider the query :

SELECT student_name FROM students WHERE 
class_name=(SELECT class_name FROM students WHERE math_marks=100);

what will be the output ?



UGC NET CSE | December 2007 | Part 2 | Question: 20
options: the list of names of students with \( 100 \) marks in mathematics
the names of all students of all classes in which at least one student has \( 100 \) marks in mathematics
the names of all students in all classes having \( 100 \) marks in mathematics
the names and class of all students whose marks in mathematics is \( 100 \)
